1â†’# Andes QEMU Usage Guide
     2â†’
     3â†’## Skill Description
     4â†’
     5â†’Provide usage examples for running QEMU with all Andes RISC-V CPUs on the `andes_ae350` machine platform.
     6â†’
     7â†’## Instructions
     8â†’
     9â†’When the user asks about QEMU usage for Andes CPUs, provide the relevant examples below. Tailor the response based on whether they need RV32 or RV64, a specific CPU model, or a general overview.
    10â†’
    11â†’## Machine
    12â†’
    13â†’The only Andes machine is `andes_ae350` (Andes AE350 platform). It supports up to 8 CPUs and a maximum of 2GB DRAM.
    14â†’
    15â†’## All Andes CPU Models
    16â†’
    17â†’### RV32 (use `qemu-system-riscv32`)
    18â†’
    19â†’| CPU (`-cpu`)    | Series | Notes                        |
    20â†’|-----------------|--------|------------------------------|
    21â†’| andes-n25       | N25    | Base integer, no FPU         |
    22â†’| andes-n25f      | N25F   | With single/double FPU       |
    23â†’| andes-n25f-se   | N25F   | With security extensions      |
    24â†’| andes-n225      | N225   | Low-power variant            |
    25â†’| andes-n45       | N45    | Superscalar, no FPU          |
    26â†’| andes-d23       | D23    | DSP-capable                  |
    27â†’| andes-d25       | D25    | DSP + base integer           |
    28â†’| andes-d25f      | D25F   | DSP + FPU                    |
    29â†’| andes-d45       | D45    | DSP superscalar              |
    30â†’| andes-a25       | A25    | Linux-capable (MMU)          |
    31â†’| andes-a27       | A27    | Linux-capable + vector       |
    32â†’| andes-a45       | A45    | Linux-capable superscalar    |
    33â†’| andes-a46mp     | A46MP  | Multiprocessor               |
    34â†’| andes-a46mpv    | A46MPV | Multiprocessor + vector      |
    35â†’
    36â†’### RV64 (use `qemu-system-riscv64`)
    37â†’
    38â†’| CPU (`-cpu`)     | Series  | Notes                       |
    39â†’|------------------|---------|-----------------------------|
    40â†’| andes-nx25       | NX25    | Base integer, no FPU        |
    41â†’| andes-nx25f      | NX25F   | With single/double FPU      |
    42â†’| andes-nx27v      | NX27V   | With vector extension        |
    43â†’| andes-nx45       | NX45    | Superscalar, no FPU         |
    44â†’| andes-nx45v      | NX45V   | Superscalar + vector        |
    45â†’| andes-ax25       | AX25    | Linux-capable (MMU)         |
    46â†’| andes-ax27       | AX27    | Linux-capable + vector      |
    47â†’| andes-ax45       | AX45    | Linux-capable superscalar   |
    48â†’| andes-ax45mpv    | AX45MPV | Multiprocessor + vector     |
    49â†’| andes-ax46mp     | AX46MP  | Multiprocessor              |
    50â†’| andes-ax46mpv    | AX46MPV | Multiprocessor + vector     |
    51â†’| andes-ax65       | AX65    | High-performance            |
    52â†’| andes-ax66       | AX66    | High-performance next-gen   |
    53â†’
    54â†’### CPU Naming Convention
    55â†’
    56â†’- **N/NX**: No MMU (bare-metal / RTOS)
    57â†’- **D**: DSP extensions (RV32 only)
    58â†’- **A/AX**: With MMU (Linux-capable)
    59â†’- **25/45/65/66**: Microarchitecture generation (45 = superscalar, 65/66 = high-perf)
    60â†’- **F** suffix: hardware FPU
    61â†’- **V** suffix: vector extension
    62â†’- **MP** suffix: multiprocessor support
    63â†’
    64â†’## Basic Usage
    65â†’
    66â†’### Minimal bare-metal run (RV64)
    67â†’
    68â†’```bash
    69â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic \
    70â†’    -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45 \
    71â†’    -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
    72â†’```
    73â†’
    74â†’### Minimal bare-metal run (RV32)
    75â†’
    76â†’```bash
    77â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic \
    78â†’    -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-a25 \
    79â†’    -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
    80â†’```
    81â†’
    82â†’### Multi-core run (up to 8 CPUs)
    83â†’
    84â†’```bash
    85â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic \
    86â†’    -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45 -smp 4 \
    87â†’    -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
    88â†’```
    89â†’
    90â†’### Using a configuration file
    91â†’
    92â†’```bash
    93â†’qemu-system-riscv64 \
    94â†’    -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx45v \
    95â†’    -readconfig docs/config/andes.cfg
    96â†’```
    97â†’
    98â†’## Common Options
    99â†’
   100â†’| Option                | Description                                       |
   101â†’|-----------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
   102â†’| `-nographic`          | Direct output to terminal (no GUI window)         |
   103â†’| `-M andes_ae350`      | Select Andes AE350 platform                       |
   104â†’| `-cpu <model>`        | Select CPU model (see tables above)               |
   105â†’| `-m 2G`               | Set memory size (max 2G for AE350)                |
   106â†’| `-smp N`              | Number of CPU cores (max 8)                       |
   107â†’| `-bios <elf>`         | Load and run an ELF binary                        |
   108â†’| `-readconfig <cfg>`   | Load machine configuration from file              |
   109â†’| `-d <items>`          | Enable debug logging (e.g., `int,cpu,in_asm`)     |
   110â†’| `-D <logfile>`        | Write debug log to file                           |
   111â†’| `-S`                  | Freeze CPU at startup (use with `-gdb`)           |
   112â†’| `-gdb tcp::<port>`    | Start GDB server on given port                    |
   113â†’| `-s`                  | Shorthand for `-gdb tcp::1234`                    |
   114â†’
   115â†’## Advanced Options
   116â†’
   117â†’### GDB debugging
   118â†’
   119â†’```bash
   120â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic \
   121â†’    -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45 \
   122â†’    -m 2G -bios firmware.elf \
   123â†’    -s -S
   124â†’```
   125â†’
   126â†’Then connect with GDB:
   127â†’
   128â†’```bash
   129â†’riscv64-unknown-elf-gdb firmware.elf -ex "target remote :1234"
   130â†’```
   131â†’
   132â†’### ILM/DLM local memory configuration
   133â†’
   134â†’```bash
   135â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic \
   136â†’    -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf \
   137â†’    -global driver=riscv.andes.ae350.soc,property=ilm_default_enable,value=on \
   138â†’    -global driver=riscv.andes.ae350.soc,property=ilm_size,value=0x10000
   139â†’```
   140â†’
   141â†’### ACE (Andes Custom Extension) library
   142â†’
   143â†’```bash
   144â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic \
   145â†’    -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx45v,xandesaceextlibpath=path/to/lib \
   146â†’    -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   147â†’```
   148â†’
   149â†’### AIA (Advanced Interrupt Architecture) with APLIC+IMSIC
   150â†’
   151â†’```bash
   152â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic \
   153â†’    -M andes_ae350,aia=aplic-imsic -cpu andes-ax45 \
   154â†’    -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   155â†’```
   156â†’
   157â†’## Run Examples for Every CPU
   158â†’
   159â†’### RV32 CPUs
   160â†’
   161â†’```bash
   162â†’# N-series (no MMU, bare-metal/RTOS)
   163â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-n25 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   164â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-n25f -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   165â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-n25f-se -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   166â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-n225 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   167â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-n45 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   168â†’
   169â†’# D-series (DSP)
   170â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-d23 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   171â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-d25 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   172â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-d25f -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   173â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-d45 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   174â†’
   175â†’# A-series (Linux-capable, with MMU)
   176â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-a25 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   177â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-a27 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   178â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-a45 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   179â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-a46mp -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   180â†’qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-a46mpv -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   181â†’```
   182â†’
   183â†’### RV64 CPUs
   184â†’
   185â†’```bash
   186â†’# NX-series (no MMU, bare-metal/RTOS)
   187â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx25 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   188â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx25f -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   189â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx27v -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   190â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx45 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   191â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx45v -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   192â†’
   193â†’# AX-series (Linux-capable, with MMU)
   194â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax25 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   195â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax27 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   196â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   197â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45mpv -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   198â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax46mp -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   199â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax46mpv -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   200â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax65 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   201â†’qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax66 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
   202â†’```
   203â†’

file.content:
# Andes QEMU Usage Guide

## Skill Description

Provide usage examples for running QEMU with all Andes RISC-V CPUs on the `andes_ae350` machine platform.

## Instructions

When the user asks about QEMU usage for Andes CPUs, provide the relevant examples below. Tailor the response based on whether they need RV32 or RV64, a specific CPU model, or a general overview.

## Machine

The only Andes machine is `andes_ae350` (Andes AE350 platform). It supports up to 8 CPUs and a maximum of 2GB DRAM.

## All Andes CPU Models

### RV32 (use `qemu-system-riscv32`)

| CPU (`-cpu`)    | Series | Notes                        |
|-----------------|--------|------------------------------|
| andes-n25       | N25    | Base integer, no FPU         |
| andes-n25f      | N25F   | With single/double FPU       |
| andes-n25f-se   | N25F   | With security extensions      |
| andes-n225      | N225   | Low-power variant            |
| andes-n45       | N45    | Superscalar, no FPU          |
| andes-d23       | D23    | DSP-capable                  |
| andes-d25       | D25    | DSP + base integer           |
| andes-d25f      | D25F   | DSP + FPU                    |
| andes-d45       | D45    | DSP superscalar              |
| andes-a25       | A25    | Linux-capable (MMU)          |
| andes-a27       | A27    | Linux-capable + vector       |
| andes-a45       | A45    | Linux-capable superscalar    |
| andes-a46mp     | A46MP  | Multiprocessor               |
| andes-a46mpv    | A46MPV | Multiprocessor + vector      |

### RV64 (use `qemu-system-riscv64`)

| CPU (`-cpu`)     | Series  | Notes                       |
|------------------|---------|-----------------------------|
| andes-nx25       | NX25    | Base integer, no FPU        |
| andes-nx25f      | NX25F   | With single/double FPU      |
| andes-nx27v      | NX27V   | With vector extension        |
| andes-nx45       | NX45    | Superscalar, no FPU         |
| andes-nx45v      | NX45V   | Superscalar + vector        |
| andes-ax25       | AX25    | Linux-capable (MMU)         |
| andes-ax27       | AX27    | Linux-capable + vector      |
| andes-ax45       | AX45    | Linux-capable superscalar   |
| andes-ax45mpv    | AX45MPV | Multiprocessor + vector     |
| andes-ax46mp     | AX46MP  | Multiprocessor              |
| andes-ax46mpv    | AX46MPV | Multiprocessor + vector     |
| andes-ax65       | AX65    | High-performance            |
| andes-ax66       | AX66    | High-performance next-gen   |

### CPU Naming Convention

- **N/NX**: No MMU (bare-metal / RTOS)
- **D**: DSP extensions (RV32 only)
- **A/AX**: With MMU (Linux-capable)
- **25/45/65/66**: Microarchitecture generation (45 = superscalar, 65/66 = high-perf)
- **F** suffix: hardware FPU
- **V** suffix: vector extension
- **MP** suffix: multiprocessor support

## Basic Usage

### Minimal bare-metal run (RV64)

```bash
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic \
    -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45 \
    -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
```

### Minimal bare-metal run (RV32)

```bash
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic \
    -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-a25 \
    -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
```

### Multi-core run (up to 8 CPUs)

```bash
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic \
    -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45 -smp 4 \
    -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
```

### Using a configuration file

```bash
qemu-system-riscv64 \
    -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx45v \
    -readconfig docs/config/andes.cfg
```

## Common Options

| Option                | Description                                       |
|-----------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
| `-nographic`          | Direct output to terminal (no GUI window)         |
| `-M andes_ae350`      | Select Andes AE350 platform                       |
| `-cpu <model>`        | Select CPU model (see tables above)               |
| `-m 2G`               | Set memory size (max 2G for AE350)                |
| `-smp N`              | Number of CPU cores (max 8)                       |
| `-bios <elf>`         | Load and run an ELF binary                        |
| `-readconfig <cfg>`   | Load machine configuration from file              |
| `-d <items>`          | Enable debug logging (e.g., `int,cpu,in_asm`)     |
| `-D <logfile>`        | Write debug log to file                           |
| `-S`                  | Freeze CPU at startup (use with `-gdb`)           |
| `-gdb tcp::<port>`    | Start GDB server on given port                    |
| `-s`                  | Shorthand for `-gdb tcp::1234`                    |

## Advanced Options

### GDB debugging

```bash
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic \
    -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45 \
    -m 2G -bios firmware.elf \
    -s -S
```

Then connect with GDB:

```bash
riscv64-unknown-elf-gdb firmware.elf -ex "target remote :1234"
```

### ILM/DLM local memory configuration

```bash
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic \
    -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf \
    -global driver=riscv.andes.ae350.soc,property=ilm_default_enable,value=on \
    -global driver=riscv.andes.ae350.soc,property=ilm_size,value=0x10000
```

### ACE (Andes Custom Extension) library

```bash
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic \
    -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx45v,xandesaceextlibpath=path/to/lib \
    -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
```

### AIA (Advanced Interrupt Architecture) with APLIC+IMSIC

```bash
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic \
    -M andes_ae350,aia=aplic-imsic -cpu andes-ax45 \
    -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
```

## Run Examples for Every CPU

### RV32 CPUs

```bash
# N-series (no MMU, bare-metal/RTOS)
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-n25 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-n25f -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-n25f-se -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-n225 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-n45 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf

# D-series (DSP)
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-d23 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-d25 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-d25f -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-d45 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf

# A-series (Linux-capable, with MMU)
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-a25 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-a27 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-a45 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-a46mp -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv32 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-a46mpv -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
```

### RV64 CPUs

```bash
# NX-series (no MMU, bare-metal/RTOS)
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx25 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx25f -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx27v -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx45 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-nx45v -m 2G -bios firmware.elf

# AX-series (Linux-capable, with MMU)
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax25 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax27 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45mpv -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax46mp -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax46mpv -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax65 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
qemu-system-riscv64 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax66 -m 2G -bios firmware.elf
```

lastPrompt: move /local/nick/qemu_andes/.claude/skills/andes-qemu-usage.md and set it as qemu-runner agent's specific skill
